title: Search API P99 latency spike — tags column missing index
description: P99 latency for /search endpoint degraded from 200ms to 4.2 seconds under normal load. Error rate increased to 12%. Users reported search results taking too long or timing out completely.
alert_text: ALERT: search-api p99_latency=4.2s threshold=2s error_rate=12% affected_users=8400
root_cause: Missing composite index on (tags, created_at) columns in the documents table. A recent migration added the tags column but the corresponding index was not created. Full table scan on 2.3M rows at each search request.
resolution: Added composite index: CREATE INDEX idx_tags_created ON documents(tags, created_at). Deployed via migration script. Latency returned to 180ms within 5 minutes of index creation.
lessons_learned: All new columns used in WHERE clauses must have corresponding indexes created in the same migration. Added index check to deployment checklist.

title: Search API unavailable — Redis connection pool exhaustion
description: Search API returning 503 errors for 100% of requests. Service health check failing. Redis connection pool completely exhausted with 500 connections in CLOSE_WAIT state.
root_cause: A code change in SearchService introduced a Redis connection leak — connections were acquired but not released in the finally block when exceptions occurred. Under load, the pool exhausted within 8 minutes of deployment.
resolution: Rolled back deployment to previous version. Fixed connection leak in SearchService by wrapping Redis operations in context manager. Increased connection pool size as temporary mitigation.
lessons_learned: Always use context managers for Redis connections. Added connection pool utilisation alert at 80% threshold.

title: Payment processing failures — third party gateway timeout
description: 20% of payment transactions failing with timeout errors. Stripe gateway returning 504 responses after 30 seconds. Customer support volume increased 300%.
root_cause: Stripe experienced a regional outage affecting eu-west-1. Our payment service had no circuit breaker implemented — every failing request waited the full 30 second timeout before failing, cascading to queue buildup.
resolution: Implemented circuit breaker pattern with 5 second timeout and fallback to queued payment mode. Payments queued during outage processed automatically when Stripe recovered.
lessons_learned: All third party integrations must have circuit breakers. Implemented Resilience4j circuit breaker across all external service calls.

title: Auth service token validation failures — certificate expiry
description: All authenticated API calls failing with 401 Unauthorized. JWT token validation failing across all services. Began exactly at midnight UTC.
root_cause: TLS certificate for the JWT signing key server expired at 00:00 UTC. Certificate renewal was configured for manual process and the renewal alert was missed. All token validation calls to the key server failed.
resolution: Renewed certificate manually. Implemented automated certificate renewal using cert-manager. Added certificate expiry monitoring with 30-day and 7-day alerts.
lessons_learned: No certificate should require manual renewal. All certificates now managed by cert-manager with automated renewal.

title: Search API slow queries — N+1 query pattern after ORM upgrade
description: Search API response times degraded from 200ms to 1.8 seconds after ORM library upgrade. Database connection count increased 400%. No errors but significant user experience degradation.
alert_text: ALERT: search-api p95_latency=1.8s threshold=1s db_connections=840 normal=200
root_cause: ORM upgrade from v2 to v3 changed default eager loading behaviour to lazy loading. Search queries that previously loaded related entities in 2 queries now executed N+1 queries — one per result row. For a search returning 50 results, this meant 51 database queries instead of 2.
resolution: Added explicit eager loading configuration to maintain v2 behaviour. Added query count assertion to integration tests to catch N+1 regressions.
lessons_learned: ORM version upgrades must include query performance regression testing. Added APM query count monitoring.

title: Payment processing high latency — database connection pool saturation
description: Payment API response times increased from 300ms to 8 seconds during peak traffic. Transactions succeeding but very slowly. Database connection pool at 100% utilisation.
root_cause: A batch reconciliation job was scheduled to run during business hours and consumed 80% of available database connections. The remaining 20% was insufficient for normal payment traffic during peak load.
resolution: Rescheduled reconciliation job to 02:00-04:00 UTC. Implemented connection pool priority queuing to give payment transactions priority over batch jobs.
lessons_learned: Batch jobs must never share connection pools with real-time transaction processing. Separate connection pools implemented for batch and transactional workloads.

title: Auth service memory leak — session objects not garbage collected
description: Auth service memory usage growing continuously from 2GB to 14GB over 6 hours until OOM kill. Service restarted automatically but issue recurred every 6-8 hours.
root_cause: Session objects were being cached in memory but the cache eviction policy was set to LRU with a maximum age of 24 hours. During high traffic, new sessions were created faster than old ones expired, causing unbounded memory growth.
resolution: Changed cache eviction to size-based (max 50,000 sessions) with LRU eviction. Added memory usage alert at 70% of limit.
lessons_learned: Memory caches must always have size-based bounds, not just time-based bounds. Time-based bounds alone are unsafe under high traffic.

title: Search API index corruption after failed migration
description: Search returning incorrect results after database migration. Some queries returning results from wrong tenants. CRITICAL — potential data privacy breach. Search disabled immediately.
root_cause: A migration script incorrectly updated tenant_id foreign keys on 14,000 rows. The migration had a WHERE clause bug that matched rows across tenant boundaries. Not caught in staging because staging only has single-tenant data.
resolution: Restored database from backup taken 2 hours before migration. Re-ran corrected migration. Added multi-tenant data validation to migration test suite.
lessons_learned: Migrations affecting tenant isolation must be tested in multi-tenant staging environment. Added automated tenant boundary checks to CI pipeline.
